blurb: Heart-shaped variegated leaves in cream, green, and rose, trailing on thin stems and flushing deep pink at the edges when happy. A dainty, colourful spiller.

care.light: Bright light with gentle sun brings out the cream-and-pink variegation; the pale, low-chlorophyll leaves scorch in harsh direct sun and turn plain green in shade.
care.water: Soak-and-dry — the thin leaves want a bit more water than fat-leaved succulents, but the mix must dry between and never stay soggy.
care.dormancy: A cool-season grower; ease off water in peak summer.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice in the growing season.
care.potting: Nice in a hanging pot; trim and replant cuttings to keep it full.
care.propagation: Stem and leaf cuttings root easily.
care.bloom: Tiny starry white flowers in summer.
care.troubles: Scorch on the variegated leaves in too much sun, rot from overwatering, and mealybugs.
care.toxicity: Considered mildly toxic to cats and dogs if eaten.

sources.title: Crassula pellucida subsp. marginalis (Calico Kitten) — RHS
